reason: GEPA pilot for Light sharpened the spell toward minimal instrumentation, ambient visibility, and explicit boundaries against surveillance, root-cause certainty, and live remediation.

updated_fields.tagline: Cast the smallest useful glow over a dark part of a system.
updated_fields.description: Use this cantrip when a system, process, or codebase has a blind spot that needs just enough illumination to become legible: a log line, a request id, a last-seen note, a README paragraph, or a tiny metric. Light does not detect hidden things, prove root cause, or trigger live remediation. It adds the smallest honest signal that helps a human orient and then stops.
when_to_use: A workflow is silent and needs one small observation point before deeper debugging or alerting work begins.
A script, runbook, or code path is opaque because one missing comment, stdout line, or doc paragraph leaves operators guessing.
A service needs a lightweight last-seen note, request identifier, or metric so humans can tell whether it is alive.
You want ambient observability or explanation without building a full dashboard, surveillance system, or paging rule.
workflow: Name the blind spot and who needs it illuminated.
Choose the lightest viable artifact: log line, comment, last-seen timestamp, request id, metric, or short doc note.
Make the signal legible, freshness-qualified where needed, and explicit about what it does not prove.
Return the artifact, where it should live, and what remains dark.
deliverables: A concrete minimal visibility artifact sized to the blind spot.
A short note on how to read it, how fresh it is, and who should use it.
A note on what remains dark and which neighboring spell fits if the user really needs discovery, highlighting, alerting, or remediation.
guardrails: Do not drift into intrusive surveillance, root-cause certainty, or live remediation.
Do not over-instrument; add only the minimum light needed and keep authorization, freshness, and ownership explicit.
updated_fields.default_prompt: Use $light to add the smallest honest log, metric, note, or indicator that makes this dark area of the system legible.

openai.short_description: Add minimal observability to a blind spot
